course.title: Institution-Grade Computing Capstone: Evidence, Impact, Operations, and Defense
course.description: Graduating learners ready to synthesize the full platform into a consequential, independently governed capstone whose technical, human, ethical, operational, and impact claims survive changed cases and an unseen defense. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a gated capstone from mastery contract and significant problem evidence through ethics, standards, outcome evaluation, feasibility, roadmap, architecture, domain invariants, security and privacy, cumulative construction, integration resilience, layered human evaluation, operations, trusted release, clean recovery, retirement, public documentation, and unseen defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate competency evidence that distinguishes personal work, assistance, collaboration, automated structure, runtime behavior, affected-user evidence, controlled operations, and remaining non-claims through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five defense milestones and a cumulative unfamiliar examination suitable for institution-grade completion review through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves the prior problem, stakeholder, affected-user, accessibility, security, privacy, evidence, revision, test, operation, recovery, contribution, and non-claim boundaries before adding complexity.

Browser labs analyze deterministic original evidence manifests only. They never execute learner shell commands, contact research participants or external services, deploy software, access secrets, or claim live accessibility, security, privacy, load, recovery, legal, or production behavior.

Passing work requires a prediction, traceable artifact, deterministic verification, changed and rejected cases, causal diagnosis, repair evidence, affected-user or stakeholder evidence where appropriate, explicit limitations, and an accountable owner.

competencies.statement: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.
misconceptions: One complex artifact automatically demonstrates every skill used to create it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.

competencies.statement: Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.
misconceptions: Disclosing tools weakens a capstone and should be minimized.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.

competencies.statement: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
misconceptions: A large quantity of screenshots creates strong evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

competencies.statement: Predefine changed-case, causal diagnosis, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, operations, and recovery questions that expose shallow mastery.
misconceptions: A prepared product walkthrough is an adequate mastery examination.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predefine changed-case, causal diagnosis, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, operations, and recovery questions that expose shallow mastery.

competencies.statement: Set proposal, implementation, validation, and defense gates that can require repair, rescope, defer, or stop before credential claims.
misconceptions: Capstone evaluators should help every started project reach approval.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set proposal, implementation, validation, and defense gates that can require repair, rescope, defer, or stop before credential claims.

competencies.statement: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.
misconceptions: Novel technology makes the addressed problem significant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.

competencies.statement: Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.
misconceptions: A broad phrase such as everyone establishes product reach.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.

competencies.statement: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.
misconceptions: Competitor feature lists explain why people use current alternatives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

competencies.statement: Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.
misconceptions: Improved product metrics prove public benefit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.

competencies.statement: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
misconceptions: Current demand guarantees a durable need.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.

competencies.statement: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.
misconceptions: Stakeholder importance is proportional to purchasing power.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

competencies.statement: Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.
misconceptions: Ethics is satisfied by legal compliance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.

competencies.statement: Treat disability, language, literacy, device, connectivity, environment, and support needs as core system requirements and research conditions.
misconceptions: Accessibility applies only to the final user interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat disability, language, literacy, device, connectivity, environment, and support needs as core system requirements and research conditions.

competencies.statement: Define prevention, detection, limit, safe failure, appeal, correction, restitution, communication, and retirement for plausible harms.
misconceptions: A terms-of-use warning transfers product harm to the user.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define prevention, detection, limit, safe failure, appeal, correction, restitution, communication, and retirement for plausible harms.

competencies.statement: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.
misconceptions: A software team can resolve every domain risk through technical controls.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.

competencies.statement: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.
misconceptions: All official-looking web pages impose equivalent requirements.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

competencies.statement: Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
misconceptions: Linking a source keeps a project current automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.

competencies.statement: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
misconceptions: Adding a disclaimer removes the need for qualified review.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

competencies.statement: Trace source, dependency, data, content, model, font, media, and generated-artifact licenses into distribution obligations.
misconceptions: Public availability means unrestricted reuse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace source, dependency, data, content, model, font, media, and generated-artifact licenses into distribution obligations.

competencies.statement: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.
misconceptions: Meeting a technical checklist permits a compliance claim.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

competencies.statement: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.
misconceptions: A feature roadmap is equivalent to a theory of change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.

competencies.statement: Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.
misconceptions: Anything quantifiable is a valid measure of the intended construct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.

competencies.statement: Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.
misconceptions: Before-and-after change proves the product caused the outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.

competencies.statement: Measure failure, exclusion, burden, privacy, security, accessibility, support, and subgroup differences alongside success.
misconceptions: Aggregate improvement means no group was harmed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure failure, exclusion, burden, privacy, security, accessibility, support, and subgroup differences alongside success.

competencies.statement: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.
misconceptions: Changing an analysis after seeing results is harmless exploration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

competencies.statement: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.
misconceptions: Motivation can compensate for every missing project dependency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.

competencies.statement: Compare build, buy, configure, integrate, simplify, manual service, policy change, partnership, and no-build alternatives.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare build, buy, configure, integrate, simplify, manual service, policy change, partnership, and no-build alternatives.

competencies.statement: Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.
misconceptions: A broad proof of concept efficiently tests all major risks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.

competencies.statement: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.
misconceptions: Implementation estimates cover the full cost of a product.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate development, research, evaluation, accessibility, security, operations, support, contingency, and retirement effort with uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.
misconceptions: Persistence despite disconfirming evidence demonstrates capstone excellence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.

competencies.statement: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.
misconceptions: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.

competencies.statement: Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.
misconceptions: Project tasks can be ordered entirely by implementation convenience.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.

competencies.statement: Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.
misconceptions: A milestone is meaningful when a component is complete.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.

competencies.statement: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.
misconceptions: Adding more parallel work always shortens the critical path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model dependencies, uncertain durations, external waits, integration points, contingency, and explicit scope-release triggers.

competencies.statement: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.
misconceptions: Task completion percentage accurately predicts capstone readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.

competencies.statement: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
misconceptions: One box-and-arrow diagram explains an architecture.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.

competencies.statement: Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
misconceptions: Architecture can maximize every quality attribute simultaneously.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.

competencies.statement: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.
misconceptions: Redundant components automatically contain failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.

competencies.statement: Plan interface, data, deployment, and organizational evolution with compatibility windows, migration, deprecation, and retirement.
misconceptions: A clean internal design makes future compatibility easy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan interface, data, deployment, and organizational evolution with compatibility windows, migration, deprecation, and retirement.

competencies.statement: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.
misconceptions: Architecture review is a presentation seeking approval.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

competencies.statement: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.
misconceptions: Types and schemas express every important invariant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.

competencies.statement: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.
misconceptions: Well-typed clients make server validation redundant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.

competencies.statement: State preconditions, invariant, termination, result, counterexample, complexity, scale boundary, and alternative for consequential algorithms.
misconceptions: Passing representative examples proves an algorithm correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State preconditions, invariant, termination, result, counterexample, complexity, scale boundary, and alternative for consequential algorithms.

competencies.statement: Specify operation semantics, status and error taxonomy, idempotency, pagination, consistency, compatibility, rate, privacy, and observation.
misconceptions: API documentation generated from routes describes complete behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify operation semantics, status and error taxonomy, idempotency, pagination, consistency, compatibility, rate, privacy, and observation.

competencies.statement: Prove backup, restore, migration, replay, reconciliation, and deletion preserve or intentionally transform domain invariants.
misconceptions: Storage-level restore success proves application recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove backup, restore, migration, replay, reconciliation, and deletion preserve or intentionally transform domain invariants.

competencies.statement: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.
misconceptions: A generic threat checklist is a project threat model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.

competencies.statement: Prove deny-by-default resource and action authorization across interface, service, data, cache, job, event, export, and support paths.
misconceptions: Successful authentication proves legitimate access.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove deny-by-default resource and action authorization across interface, service, data, cache, job, event, export, and support paths.

competencies.statement: Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.
misconceptions: Privacy risk equals cybersecurity risk to stored personal data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.

competencies.statement: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden.
misconceptions: Blocking known malicious strings prevents product abuse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden.

competencies.statement: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.
misconceptions: Applying a patch closes a vulnerability incident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define reporting, triage, severity, containment, remediation, disclosure, dependency response, verification, and learning ownership.

competencies.statement: Build the smallest end-to-end path through authentic interface, domain, data, deployment, telemetry, accessibility, security, and recovery boundaries.
misconceptions: A mocked demo is enough to validate the delivery system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build the smallest end-to-end path through authentic interface, domain, data, deployment, telemetry, accessibility, security, and recovery boundaries.

competencies.statement: For each increment, retrieve prior requirements, predict behavior, implement, test accepted and rejected cases, validate with users, and update claims.
misconceptions: Small commits automatically create cumulative learning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: For each increment, retrieve prior requirements, predict behavior, implement, test accepted and rejected cases, validate with users, and update claims.

competencies.statement: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
misconceptions: Quality can be accumulated in a final hardening phase.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.

competencies.statement: Reproduce a defect, preserve evidence, compare hypotheses, identify the first invalid transition, repair cause, add regression, and verify changed cases.
misconceptions: The line changed by a fix identifies the root cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce a defect, preserve evidence, compare hypotheses, identify the first invalid transition, repair cause, add regression, and verify changed cases.

competencies.statement: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt.
misconceptions: Visible duplication always justifies immediate abstraction.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use measured design pressure and protected behavior to choose local refactor, boundary extraction, replacement, or deliberate debt.

competencies.statement: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

competencies.statement: Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
misconceptions: Using the official SDK guarantees integration correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.

competencies.statement: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

competencies.statement: Provide bounded queueing, fallback, cached or reduced behavior, accessible status, safe stop, and recovery when a dependency degrades.
misconceptions: A circuit breaker is a complete resilience strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide bounded queueing, fallback, cached or reduced behavior, accessible status, safe stop, and recovery when a dependency degrades.

competencies.statement: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.
misconceptions: An interface abstraction makes vendor exit inexpensive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

competencies.statement: Map behavior, quality, risk, outcome, and impact claims to unit, property, contract, integration, system, fault, human, and production evidence.
misconceptions: Every important claim should be covered by an automated test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map behavior, quality, risk, outcome, and impact claims to unit, property, contract, integration, system, fault, human, and production evidence.

competencies.statement: Generate boundary, malformed, hostile, scale, accessibility, concurrency, failure, and environment cases that differ structurally from teaching examples.
misconceptions: Random inputs automatically create meaningful adversarial tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate boundary, malformed, hostile, scale, accessibility, concurrency, failure, and environment cases that differ structurally from teaching examples.

competencies.statement: Run representative tasks with neutral protocol, accommodations, observation, success criteria, breakdown capture, consent, and uncertainty.
misconceptions: Users who eventually finish a task validate usability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run representative tasks with neutral protocol, accommodations, observation, success criteria, breakdown capture, consent, and uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Combine automated, keyboard, zoom, contrast, content, screen-reader or structured-text, and disabled-user evidence without unsupported conformance claims.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine automated, keyboard, zoom, contrast, content, screen-reader or structured-text, and disabled-user evidence without unsupported conformance claims.

competencies.statement: Match the strength, population, environment, duration, causality, and certainty of each public claim to the collected evidence.
misconceptions: Cautious wording can make weak evidence acceptable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Match the strength, population, environment, duration, causality, and certainty of each public claim to the collected evidence.

competencies.statement: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
misconceptions: A successful load test reveals production capacity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
misconceptions: Infrastructure health metrics are sufficient service indicators.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.

competencies.statement: Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.
misconceptions: Recording complete request context is the safest diagnostic approach.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.

competencies.statement: Create symptom, impact, evidence, decision, mitigation, escalation, rollback, recovery, communication, and verification runbooks tested by another operator.
misconceptions: A list of commands is an actionable runbook.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create symptom, impact, evidence, decision, mitigation, escalation, rollback, recovery, communication, and verification runbooks tested by another operator.

competencies.statement: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
misconceptions: Restoring the happy path completes an incident exercise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

competencies.statement: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.
misconceptions: A dependency scanner inventories the whole product.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.

competencies.statement: Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.
misconceptions: A signed artifact is necessarily trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.

competencies.statement: Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.
misconceptions: Blue-green infrastructure removes deployment risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.

competencies.statement: Restore into an isolated environment from documented backups, rotate secrets, verify invariants and user workflows, measure recovery objectives, and preserve evidence.
misconceptions: Testing restore over the existing environment proves disaster recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Restore into an isolated environment from documented backups, rotate secrets, verify invariants and user workflows, measure recovery objectives, and preserve evidence.

competencies.statement: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.
misconceptions: A capstone can be abandoned after assessment without lifecycle risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.

competencies.statement: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
misconceptions: One comprehensive README serves every audience well.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.
misconceptions: A flawless happy-path demo is the most persuasive evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.

competencies.statement: Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.
misconceptions: Memorizing architecture vocabulary demonstrates independent mastery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.

competencies.statement: Publish selective problem, role, constraints, decisions, implementation, validation, results, limitations, and links without sensitive data or inflated impact.
misconceptions: A portfolio case study should maximize perceived success.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish selective problem, role, constraints, decisions, implementation, validation, results, limitations, and links without sensitive data or inflated impact.

competencies.statement: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.
misconceptions: Reflection is a chronological summary of completed work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare initial predictions with outcomes, explain changed mental models, identify retained misconceptions, and design a harder independent transfer task.

credential.title: Institution-Grade Computing Capstone: Evidence, Impact, Operations, and Defense Certificate

modules.title: Capstone Mastery Contract and Evidence Architecture
modules.description: A learner proposes an impressive build but cannot explain which curriculum outcomes it will assess, what independent evidence will count, or how overclaiming will be prevented. Learners cumulatively produce capstone competency and evidence architecture through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.
Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.
Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.

modules.title: Significant Problem, Public Value, and Durable Need
modules.description: A novel technical idea lacks evidence of a consequential recurring problem, reachable affected people, existing alternatives, or value beyond demonstration. Learners cumulatively produce significance and public-value case through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.
Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.
Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.

modules.title: Stakeholders, Ethics, Accessibility, and Harm Analysis
modules.description: Primary-user convenience may shift surveillance, labor, exclusion, safety, environmental, or procedural costs onto less visible people. Learners cumulatively produce stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.
Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.
Treat disability, language, literacy, device, connectivity, environment, and support needs as core system requirements and research conditions.

modules.title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries
modules.description: The product depends on health, finance, education, civic, creative, or safety assumptions inferred from secondary summaries and incompatible licenses. Learners cumulatively produce domain authority and compliance boundary dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.
Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.

modules.title: Outcome Measurement, Baselines, Guardrails, and Evaluation Design
modules.description: The capstone promises impact but relies on vanity metrics, no counterfactual, unclear instrumentation, and post hoc interpretation. Learners cumulatively produce outcome evaluation and measurement protocol through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.
Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.
Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.

modules.title: Feasibility, Alternatives, Risk Experiments, and Stop Decisions
modules.description: A long capstone plan assumes access to data, users, infrastructure, expertise, approvals, integrations, time, and operating budget that have not been tested. Learners cumulatively produce feasibility proof and alternative decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.
Compare build, buy, configure, integrate, simplify, manual service, policy change, partnership, and no-build alternatives.
Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.

modules.title: Capstone Scope, Dependency Graph, Milestones, and Risk Control
modules.description: The plan is organized by components and deadlines rather than prerequisite evidence, risk retirement, end-to-end value, and independent assessment gates. Learners cumulatively produce evidence-ordered capstone delivery system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.
Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.
Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.

modules.title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance
modules.description: The capstone architecture diagram shows components but not quality scenarios, trust boundaries, failure containment, evolution, cost, or rejected alternatives. Learners cumulatively produce evaluated architecture and decision governance packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.

modules.title: Data, API, Algorithm, and Domain Invariants
modules.description: The product has schemas and endpoints but no proof of identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, algorithmic, lifecycle, or compatibility correctness. Learners cumulatively produce executable domain and interface assurance suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.
Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.
State preconditions, invariant, termination, result, counterexample, complexity, scale boundary, and alternative for consequential algorithms.

modules.title: Threat Modeling, Privacy Engineering, and Abuse-Resistant Design
modules.description: Security and privacy are represented by authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page without system-specific adversarial evidence. Learners cumulatively produce threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace assets, actors, entry points, data flows, trust boundaries, attack paths, controls, detection, response, and residual risk.
Prove deny-by-default resource and action authorization across interface, service, data, cache, job, event, export, and support paths.
Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.

modules.title: Walking Skeleton, Core Domain, and Cumulative Construction
modules.description: The capstone has extensive infrastructure and design artifacts but no small deployed path proving that user value and all critical engineering gates can coexist. Learners cumulatively produce deployed cumulative capstone core through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build the smallest end-to-end path through authentic interface, domain, data, deployment, telemetry, accessibility, security, and recovery boundaries.
For each increment, retrieve prior requirements, predict behavior, implement, test accepted and rejected cases, validate with users, and update claims.
Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.

modules.title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience
modules.description: The product relies on APIs, queues, models, identity, payments, files, or notifications whose delays, duplication, outages, drift, and policy changes are hidden. Learners cumulatively produce controlled integration and resilience evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

modules.title: Layered Verification, Human Evaluation, and Claim Calibration
modules.description: A large automated suite passes, but test data is familiar, accessibility is tool-only, human tasks are scripted, and evidence does not map to impact claims. Learners cumulatively produce multi-method capstone evaluation dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map behavior, quality, risk, outcome, and impact claims to unit, property, contract, integration, system, fault, human, and production evidence.
Generate boundary, malformed, hostile, scale, accessibility, concurrency, failure, and environment cases that differ structurally from teaching examples.
Run representative tasks with neutral protocol, accommodations, observation, success criteria, breakdown capture, consent, and uncertainty.

modules.title: Capacity, Observability, Support, and Production Operations
modules.description: The capstone is feature-complete but has no realistic workload, user-centered service measures, operator workflow, support path, capacity boundary, or incident rehearsal. Learners cumulatively produce operational readiness and capacity case through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.

modules.title: Supply Chain, Deployment, Migration, Backup, and Recovery
modules.description: The release candidate has unclear source-to-artifact identity, unreviewed components, mutable environments, untested migrations, and backup success without restore proof. Learners cumulatively produce release integrity and clean recovery proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory source, dependencies, tools, generated code, models, data, images, services, licenses, vulnerabilities, and maintainers with current ownership.
Bind protected source and workflow, isolated build, declared inputs, immutable output, SBOM, provenance, signature or attestation, and verification.
Move one reviewed artifact through compatible migration, readiness, bounded exposure, guardrails, hold, abort, promotion, and user communication.

modules.title: Documentation, Demonstration, Independent Defense, and Reflective Transfer
modules.description: A polished final presentation shows the product but hides failed hypotheses, human evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, contribution limits, and what remains unproven. Learners cumulatively produce public capstone dossier and independent defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
Demonstrate ordinary, changed, boundary, inaccessible-before-repair, failure, recovery, rollback, and stakeholder-outcome cases from the reviewed artifact.
Answer unseen causal, counterexample, tradeoff, ethics, accessibility, security, data, operations, and transfer questions using inspectable evidence.
